mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or out-of-sample accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both grow, with a hit rate below 50% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below zero during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending earnings revisions, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) outside early-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, ultra-loose monetary policy compresses equity risk premiums and forces portfolio rebalancing into stocks, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields a positive return hit rate below 50% or a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate profit margins, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal consumption expenditures accelerate indicates unsustainable consumer borrowing that precedes a demand collapse, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases is followed by negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance during expansion regimes.
